title: ASSIGNMENT 1
text:
This is one of the most popular application in the US. It has actively collected and taken advantages of millions of their customers, including me, to advance their revenue. They have analysed my history purchases, and search to manipulate my shopping habit.
The history purchase provide them which products, manufacturers, price range, and categories I preferred to spend money on. Also, the time I usually went window shopping and decided to make payment is exposed to Amazon. According to those information, whenever I log into my Amazon account, the algorithms shows me a list of products, which they believe I will be interested to buy.



Like Amazon, Facebook has collected my footprint on social media since I created an account on their platform. Everything I do on Facebook, from every pages or group I followed to every single thread I interacted via emocons or comments, gives out my personal references. It includes political bias, favorite images, destinations, and so on.
However, unlike Amazon, Facebook usually sell my personal data to marketing firms. Those companies used those data to create a profile matching with all my preference. As a result, they can send out customized advertising campaigns or digital contents which might influence my decisions in their favor.



Netflix has dominated streaming services for ages. Their revenue comes from monthly subscriptions around the world. The more users make commitment to their service every month, the more profit Netflix receive. Accordingly, they have analyzed my watching patterns.

What types of movies or shows have I picked? Movies? Documentary series? Reality shows?
What languages have I chosen for movies? with or without subtitle?
Which category have I preferred? Comedy? Horror? Fantasy?

All of those help them to offer me good recommendation, or plan a reasonable purchase in future. On my end, I might find it satisfied with the Netflix library and continue to pay monthly subscription.

title: ASSIGNMENT 1
text:
Healthcare Management

Purpose
The application addresses if the medical care providers or facilities are in the network. It might help the insured know which charges are eligible for FSA reimbursement.


Functions

Target audience? function to serve that audience?
List of medical facilities within the network, including ER and Emergency rooms
List of medical care providers, including doctors, specialists (dermatologist, anesthesiologist, etc)
List of FSA qualified items and operations.



Interface Design

Input insurance plan (providers, plan type, account number)
Input personal information
Input facility location
Input operation date and type

title: ASSIGNMENT 2
text:
Relation schema defines the logical structure of a table (relation) in a database without actual data. It describes the columns (attributes) also.
Unlike relation instance, relation schema usually does not change over time.
Example: university (school : string, building : string, contact : string)



A relation represents an actual table in a database. It is made up of a group of rows (tuples), following the structure defined by the relation schema.
Example: university (school, building, email)



School
Building
Contact




EPPS
Green Hall
469-123-4567


Engineering
Erik Josson
972-123-4567






Relation instance is a set of rows (tuples) in a given time frame. It may change with time as the relation is updated.
Example: The university table has two records as instance at this moment. If the table gets updated (addition, modification, or deletion), the instance will change.



School
Building
Contact

Question 1

Strong entity set can be identified by its own super key. Example: police geography table is uniquely identified by Reporting_Area values.



Reporting_Area
Beat
Sector
Division

Central



Weak entity set doesn’t have unique column to identify itself. The table relies on a strong entity set for identification. Example: recovered_veh table needs to use VIN from stolen_veh table to identify if the recovered vehicle is associated with any stolen vehicle incident across the city of Dallas.
